findings.evidence: `Regular.lean:84` has `variable {R C : Type u}`; nearby existing API `Trivial.lean:300` has `variable (R : Type u) (C : Type v) [CommSemiring R] [Semiring C] [Bialgebra R C]`.
findings.fix: Use separate binders, e.g. `variable {R : Type u} {C : Type v} [CommSemiring R] [Semiring C] [Bialgebra R C]`, matching `Comodule.trivial` and `ComoduleCat.trivial`.
findings.issue: `Comodule.Hom.trivialToRegular` and its lemmas bind `variable {R C : Type u}`, unnecessarily forcing the bialgebra coalgebra into the same universe as the base.

findings.evidence: `Regular.lean:43` and `Regular.lean:120` use separate `R : Type u`, `C : Type v`, but `Regular.lean:142` and `Regular.lean:162` use `variable {R C : Type u}`.
findings.fix: Change the bundled `GroupLike` and `Bialgebra` sections to separate universe binders `{R : Type u} {C : Type v}` and keep the result type `regular R C`.
findings.issue: `ComoduleCat.groupLikeToRegular` and the bundled bialgebra section also restrict `R` and `C` to one universe, even though `ComoduleCat.regular` and the unbundled `groupLikeToRegular` are universe-polymorphic.

summary: Several new morphism APIs unnecessarily require the base ring and coalgebra to live in the same universe, unlike the surrounding comodule and trivial-comodule APIs. Generalize those binders to preserve the natural `R : Type u`, `C : Type v` level.
